item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the original source has been lost and no source found indicates it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m confused as to why this is Safe class. It’s a whole bunch of animals that need a habitat to contain. They could potentially breach containment, and the procedures even list protocols that should be followed if such an event occurs. They need to be fed as well. Shouldn’t this be Euclid class?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Smapti apparently had a burst of great creative success in 2012. I miss him.
As a writer of fiction myself I can relate. It's almost like I have no control over when it comes and it's like a flash flood.
I WONDER how it is with him.

This is one of,my all time favorites. Thank you Smapti.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reading some of the early comments on here was a good reminder of how much the site's culture was still in a transitory period when I first joined. This is such a wonderful, charming article, and makes me glad that we were allowed to evolve beyond pure horror or squick.

People say this is Redwall, but it's not. It's goddamn Squarriors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember reading this before I became a site member. It stuck with me for a while, and I have only just rediscovered it now. This is masterfully crafted, with the hooks and how the whole society works. Great work here.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I imagine they're looking for Duke Baxter and his band of Protestant ne'er-do-wells, but haven't managed to find them just yet. There are millions of acres of forest in the Pacific Northwest for his stragglers to hide in, and they probably haven't had any reason to intrude into populated areas now that King Eugenio's forces have been removed from power.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wouldn't it be in the foundation's best interest to capture "Duke Baxter"? Allowing it to roam freely could cause an incident similar to the one that warranted The Foundation's attention in the first place. Even if something like that doesn't happen, someone will be bound to notice a civilized society like that eventually.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't suppose that you've gotten around to writing a tale about this, have you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going to do my first SCP based on "Aesop", a Pyramid Magazine article I wrote, but Steve Jackson Games did not give me permission. Then I came across this SCP, which is done much more enchantingly than I would have done (my version would have been darker). Favorite one I saw so far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Passion of the Christ may be because the foundation doesn't want them to learn how to crucify people, although more likely they don't want to give them a tv for security reasons, and why bother giving them a dvd player and disc when the two are useless without the other.

In regards to coffee, they probably hear the researchers talk about it and want some. As stated earlier…do you want these guys to be caffeinated?

Leviathan is a book that promotes unified government to prevent strife and domestic conflict which in turn can lead to civil war (how they were discovered, and a threat to containment). Prince on the other hand looks at the darker side of politics-cunning, deceiving nature, manipulation. These things can put containment at risk if they begin to use it against the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
YES.

There's a CK2 mod set in post-apocalyptic America, right? We should get them to work this in somehow XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is and almost certainly always will be my favourite skip. Probably because of my political and religious biases, but oh well. Plus the sheer awesomeness/hilarity of a Catholic feudal animal kingdom. The request log cracked me up (especially the priestly garments and the political marriage, and Goodnight Moon was heartwarming, for some reason), though some parts don't quite make sense. Why liturgical music and chants, but not Passion of the Christ? Why can't the poor animals have coffee beans (and for that matter, why *do* they want coffee)? Why Leviathan but not The Prince? Why not the Song of Roland? Why not the childcare book? And, if I'm not wrong, the idea of the divine right of kings was more predominant in the late-feudal/early-absolutist period, where power was shifting from the nobles and gentry to the king, and used to justify said centralisation of power. The divine nature of authority had been around for far longer, but IIRC the term "divine right of kings" only saw currency in the absolutist period, used to place the king above the aristocracy (and in Protestant countries, above the Church). But aside from these little quibbles, this is an amazing skip. Well done, sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it but I cant put a finger on why. I gave it a +1, but I cant articulate my reasons, I just liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for the painting. Seriously, how does a person do that!?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good concept, perfectly executed, get better and better with every paragraph. I wanted to congratulate you on this, it's certainly exemplary.

My only complain is that now, I want a mod for EU4 or Cruader Kings based on this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, Purina DOES make foods for most animals. The more wild varieties are made and distributed under Mazuri (including bear food and rhino licks), a subsidiary. http://www.mazuri.com/product_pdfs/ProductList-Alpha.pdf would be a current list of products. There's no reason the foundation can't feed any animal population.

P.S. I KNOW this is necro, but had to point out that there is indeed a form of Purina <insert critter> chow for most every species.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this so much. The fact that we have some of the scariest fiction around, absolutely brilliant sci-fi, and then articles like this that are full of weird and whimsy anchored by a solid idea, all in one place, is what really makes this the best creative collaboration I've ever found. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Coffee-FLAVORED ice cream.
There's no concern regarding the flavor. The flavor is fine for them.
But do YOU want to see what these guys could do after some caffeine? Probably not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one if definitely among my favorites.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, I love this SCP, but I've always wondered.

Why would they be denied ground coffee but allowed coffee ice cream?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: good point…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps that's exactly what it is?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed reading this SCP, but one major red flag that continues to bother me is that the king is apparently a Catholic but styles himself "Defender of the Faith." With one very brief exception (Pope Leo's short-lived endorsement of Henry VIII), this title has been exclusively associated with opposition to Roman Catholicism in England. It seems like a Tudor-era anachronism when many of the kingdom's other traits imply a context several centuries earlier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, you just get put on a list or two.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"We of the royal house prefer not to be affronted by this abominable song any longer." ?

As an aside, I was surprised not to see more of Monmouth or any Mallory listed here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Will I get hurt if I ask what does the fox say?

item.title: New headcanon
item.content:encoded:
I really like this one. It nicely illustrates what a weird, and yet rational, universe the Foundation lives in.

I have to say that, in my headcanon, the rebel deer is, in actuality, Saturn Deer, out sowing chaos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Holy water, Communion wafers and wine, and priestly vestments tailored to fit a deer."

Fucking upvoted.

I like that the Foundation has found a group of talking animals out of some sort of very devout fairy tale, and immediately decided to lock them all up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 1006 isn't giant spiders. It's ordinary-sized spiders.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The difference between this and 1006 is that 1006 is about giant spiders, with them being communist just for some WTF. This goes into detail.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay here's an idea

How about you don't

pls

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not gonna say that it's a sign, but it's at least a sign-like occurrence.

:edit:

Eye kan spel gud.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And now the new pope's name is Francis.

Guess I'm gonna have to write that tale after all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I did enjoy this but I have to ask, how exactly do these animals create/use the tools? Do they have thumbs or is this all done oraly? I think maybe some bears could do a bit though not much. And I didn't see any monkeys. The one racoon I can understand using pen and paper but the rest of them…especially the birds.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved all of this, but the requests list was what got my upvote. Well done.

This sort of article is what keeps me coming back to the site—it's compelling and well-written. The scary stuff is nice, but this is just freaking cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someone had better tell that to all the foxes and deer that live in the Pacific Temperate Rainforest. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"habitat simulating a North American rainforest"
If I remember correctly, there are no rainforests in north america with foxes and deer in them. I think it would be a temperate forest. Other than that, I liked it, especially the request for vestments tailored for a deer.

item.title: Re: Hmmm
item.content:encoded: The way I figure it, Duke Baxter had fled the field when the Foundation forces attacked (thus his not being accounted for among the survivors or the dead), and the two factions pretty much coalesced under Eugenio attempting to fight off the humans until they realized they couldn't win and he surrendered.

item.title: Hmmm
item.content:encoded:
I just noticed something very interesting.

"After the death of █ MTF members and ██ animals, the surviving animals of both factions, following a loud vocalization by SCP-1845-1, ceased attack and "surrendered", allowing themselves to be taken into Foundation custody"

Eugenio was able to make the survivors on both sides surrender? If you're engaged in a civil war, would you listen to the enemy commander when he says "the aliens have arrived and we must surrender to them" ?

Just how much control does he have over his followers?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They have not yet responded, come on, these things take time after all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That just…made my day…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: actuallly, yes, a haft is an axe handle…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This would have to be one of my favorite SCP's

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A shrewed O5 would have accepted the offer of vassalage, used SCP-1845-1 to capture the "Protestant" deer and his forces, then established a new containment area on "1845-1's lands".
But then, maybe our O5s aren't so adept at medieval diplomacy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Does it think it is Human?

No.

If not, does it think itself to have original sin?

Yes.

Does it know about all Papal declarations and councils up until now, it's capture, or a certain cut-off date?

It knows of everything up until some point in the Renaissance, and only bits and pieces of what came after. It only learned about Vatican II fairly recently.

Is it a YEC or Theistic evolutionist?

Probably the former.

Can I talk to it?
Please?
PLEASEEEEE???

I haven't even caught up with all of Ask Lord Blackwood yet. Ask King Eugenio is gonna have to wait. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a Catholic with some interest in theology I have some questions.

Does it think it is Human?
If not, does it think itself to have original sin?
Does it know about all Papal declarations and councils up until now, it's capture, or a certain cut-off date?
Is it a YEC or Theistic evolutionist?
Can I talk to it?
Please?
PLEASEEEEE???

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"The Ninety-Five Theses, Martin Luther. Granted. Document was destroyed by SCP-1845-1 in a public ceremony."

I loled.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i decided to upvote after read the description but when i saw this

A political marriage to O5-█'s daughter. Denied.

+1(2)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Considering that Leviticus is OT and is specifically about the Jewish priesthood, and since the Catholic Church has no rules I know of against allowing cripples/midgets/eunuchs/etc. from becoming priests, I doubt it would bother them too much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this SCP. I'unno if it's my natural bias (being a furfag and all) or what, but there's something about Eugino and his 'Kingdom' that I like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1845-3 isn't a tiger, it's the collection of animals that make up the 'serf' class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It would also be interesting to see how the animal clergy tries to deal with Leviticus 21:16-23:

"16 The Lord said to Moses, 17 “Say to Aaron: ‘For the generations to come none of your descendants who has a defect may come near to offer the food of his God. 18 No man who has any defect may come near: no man who is blind or lame, disfigured or deformed; 19 no man with a crippled foot or hand, 20 or who is a hunchback or a dwarf, or who has any eye defect, or who has festering or running sores or damaged testicles. 21 No descendant of Aaron the priest who has any defect is to come near to present the food offerings to the Lord. He has a defect; he must not come near to offer the food of his God. 22 He may eat the most holy food of his God, as well as the holy food; 23 yet because of his defect, he must not go near the curtain or approach the altar, and so desecrate my sanctuary. I am the Lord, who makes them holy. ’”

Does being a non-human animal count as being "disfigured or deformed" and thus constitute ineligibility for the priesthood?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am making the 100th post in this article's thread to mark the occasion of this SCP hitting +100.

Here's to the little fox that could. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not going to delete it, but I am going to ask that this doesn't turn into another argument.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Burma. Duh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Where'd the Burmese Tiger come from?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Late-ish reply, but I take exception to the following:

Yes we don't kill stuff, but it also doesn't make sense to keep everything for the sake of keeping, which isn't profitable or beneficial (of course, It's my opinion, since I prefer realism to coolness)

Yes it is.

We are not the GOC. We study SCPs to try to figure out how they work. We keep everything because you just fucking never know when you're gonna need either it, or more importantly by far, when we'll need the knowledge we can get from understanding it.

Also: "Grimdark" doesn't equal "realistic". There is nothing more realistic about the vast majority of other SCPs compared to this. Just say it breaks your suspension of disbelief and leave it at that.

(I grant you that I don't buy them killing so many MTFers either, but that has since been edited down to between 1 and 9 (one blackbox), which I can buy.)

edit: hehehehe, I totally missed the last page of discussion. oops!

edit: And now I got to the mod post asking for an end to this sort of discussion. If this post violates that order, any mod feel free to delete it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Odd choices on some of the "Approve/deny" list.

Notes:

Only one animal other than SCP-1845-1 has been observed to understand written language - a raccoon belonging to SCP-1845-3, which has been observed using a quill pen and ink to take dictation, in Latin, from SCP-1845-1. SCP-1845-3 has refused to write anything under instructions from members of staff.

Well, as SCP-1845-3 is a tiger, I'm not surprised.

During a "pitched battle" between the armies, the MTF struck.

That's not a very clinical description…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Realized that I never reversed my downvote after the overhaul. Rectified.
